problem: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared?
mechanism: Stem cell differentiation into target tissue type
hypothesis: Why do amyloid plaques form in Alzheimer's and can they be cleared? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Stem. Stem cell differentiation into target tissue type directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Randomized controlled trial Phase I with 33 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.
